paper_title: Microarray and RHE Inflammation Model Analysis

methods.method_name: Microarray Analysis of PM-Induced Inflammation in RHE
methods.description: This method involves analyzing gene expression changes in response to particulate matter (PM) exposure using microarray technology. It aims to identify up-regulated and down-regulated genes and pathways involved in inflammation.

steps.description: Fold changes in gene expression levels in PM-exposed samples from those in the control were shown using a heat map.
steps.searchable_summary: generate heat map of gene expression fold changes

steps.description: Ranking based on the pathway enrichment analysis by MetaCore software.
steps.searchable_summary: perform pathway enrichment analysis using MetaCore

methods.method_name: Evaluation of Protection by N-W/O Using the RHE Inflammation Model
methods.description: This method evaluates the protective effects of N-W/O and C-W/O formulations on RHE samples exposed to urban dust or cedar pollen, using viability assays and histological observations.

steps.description: The viability of RHE samples after 24 h exposure to urban dust or cedar pollen with the application of no treatment, the base formula, N-W/O and C-W/O was measured using the MTT assay.
steps.searchable_summary: measure RHE sample viability using MTT assay

steps.description: MMP1 and IL-8 secretion in the culture media significantly increased after 24-h exposure to urban dust and cedar pollen.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze MMP1 and IL-8 secretion levels

steps.description: Histological observations of RHE, a thickened stratum corneum and thinned vital layer were observed in the 24-h PM-exposed groups by H&E staining.
steps.searchable_summary: perform histological analysis using H&E staining

steps.description: Immunohistochemical analysis with the E-cadherin antibody.
